title: Python: add packet-level telemetry to SimRadio and lichen-sim
description:
Add comprehensive telemetry to Python simulator and SimRadio client.

## Files to modify:
- python/src/lichen/radio/sim_client.py - SimRadio class
- python/src/lichen/sim/simulation.py - Simulation class  
- python/src/lichen/sim/node.py - SimNode class
- python/src/lichen/sim/metrics.py - Metrics class (exists, extend it)

## Requirements:

### 1. Packet logging in SimRadio
In SimRadio.transmit() and receive():
```python
logger.info('tx', node_id=self.node_id, len=len(payload), hex=payload.hex(), 
            packet_hash=hashlib.sha256(payload).hexdigest()[:16])
logger.info('rx', node_id=self.node_id, len=len(data), hex=data.hex(),
            rssi=rssi, snr=snr, packet_hash=hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()[:16])
```

### 2. Per-node metrics
Add to SimNode or create NodeMetrics:
```python
@dataclass
class NodeMetrics:
    tx_count: int = 0
    rx_count: int = 0
    tx_bytes: int = 0
    rx_bytes: int = 0
    unique_peers: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)  # IIDs seen
    errors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    packet_hashes_sent: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
    packet_hashes_received: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
```

### 3. Pcap capture
lichen-sim already has pcap_writer - ensure it captures ALL packets with proper timestamps.
Check python/src/lichen/sim/pcap.py exists and is wired up.

### 4. JSON metrics export
Add to Simulation:
```python
def export_metrics(self, path: Path) -> None:
    '''Export per-node metrics as JSON for analysis.'''
    metrics = {node_id: node.metrics.to_dict() for node_id, node in self._nodes.items()}
    path.write_text(json.dumps(metrics, indent=2))
```

### 5. Cross-impl tracking
The packet_hash (sha256[:16]) allows matching:
- Python TX hash X -> check if Rust/Zephyr RX has hash X
- Post-test analysis script can diff the hash sets

## Acceptance:
- pytest tests/sim/test_cross_impl_interop.py shows packet hashes in logs
- Metrics JSON written at end of test
- Pcap file contains all packets
